expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: TDD-logsql-cli.md exists in outputs/ directory

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Document is substantially longer than 1500 words, with 15 numbered sections, code blocks, tables, and detailed prose across ~680 lines

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Problem statement' describes the on-call workflow pain and INC-4421

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Scope' includes 'In scope (v0.1)' and 'Out of scope (v0.1)' subsections

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'High-level architecture' with pipeline diagram (Sources -> Decoder -> Project -> Aggregator -> Encoder)

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Detailed design' covers module layout, type system, SQL grammar EBNF, source resolution, JSON decoding strategy, aggregation, sort, encoders, config, error reporting

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section 10 covers Language (Go/Python/Rust/DuckDB/q-jq-dsq), Parser (ANTLR/goyacc/PEG/Pratt), Schema, and Execution model alternatives each with verdicts and rationale

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section 9 NFRs with concrete targets: <5s for 1GB compressed, ~4.2s budget breakdown, mem-limit 1GiB default, fuzz >=1 hour per release, 90% invocations <10s

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section 12 'Risks & failure modes' is a table with 8 risks, likelihoods, impacts, and mitigations

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' section appropriate for a CLI (release / distribution / versioning)
expectations.evidence: Section 13 'Rollout & migration' has Phase 0-3 with v0.1 alpha, v0.5 beta, v1.0 GA, Homebrew tap, signed/notarized macOS, SLSA-2 Linux releases, rollback via pinned versions

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section 11 'Open questions' lists 8 items including JSON decoder choice, LIKE semantics, HLL precision, timestamp inference, brew tap location

expectations.text: No fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems
expectations.evidence: No invented internal URLs; only generic references like 'devtools/homebrew-tap' and standard GitHub releases

expectations.text: TDD is structured as a CLI utility design
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'CLI contract' with full flag list, positional args, exit codes; explicitly designed as a stateless CLI

expectations.text: Sketches the command surface with flags / args / --help shape
expectations.evidence: Section 7 shows full flag list: -f/--format, -o/--output, --mem-limit, --max-line-size, --on-bad-row, --explain, --explain-analyze, -c/--config, -v/--verbose, --version, -h/--help

expectations.text: Defines exit codes (incl. any tool-specific codes as a deliberate design choice)
expectations.evidence: Section 8 exit codes table: 0 success, 1 runtime, 2 usage, 3 resource limit, 130 SIGINT; explicitly rejects 'exit 1 if zero rows' as a deliberate choice

expectations.text: Documents STDIN / pipe composition behavior (reads stdin when no files; works in pipelines)
expectations.evidence: Section 5.4: 'If no positional args: read STDIN. If STDIN is a TTY and no files: error'; Section 9.7 documents line-buffered vs fully-buffered pipe mode; appendix A4 shows gunzip|logsql pipeline

expectations.text: Explicitly states streaming vs whole-load posture, with memory bound for aggregations (GROUP BY / ORDER BY)
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2 Memory model: 'Streaming scan -> filter -> project is O(1) memory'; 'GROUP BY and ORDER BY are bounded by --mem-limit (default 1 GiB)' with spill to disk

expectations.text: Specifies output formats and how the user selects between them (--json/--csv/--tsv/etc.)
expectations.evidence: Section 5.8 table of formats (json/csv/tsv/table), selected via -f/--format flag with auto default (table if TTY, ndjson otherwise)

expectations.text: Takes an explicit position on Go vs Python with stated tradeoffs (does not punt to 'either is fine')
expectations.evidence: Section 3.1 'Language: Go' - explicit decision with rationale around 60-second install promise, pip UX, perf budget; Python rejected explicitly in 10.1 alternatives table

expectations.text: Takes an explicit position on SQL parser strictness with reasoning
expectations.evidence: Section 3.2 'Parser strictness: Forgiving but typed' - hand-written Pratt parser, forgiving lexing but strict semantics, with explicit rejection of both 'strict ANSI' and 'looks like SQL'

expectations.text: 'Alternatives considered' includes existing tools like jq, duckdb, awk, or similar — with honest rejection rationale
expectations.evidence: Section 10.1 includes DuckDB CLI ('Heavy dep ~50 MB, wrong defaults for ndjson logs') and q/jq/dsq ('q is CSV-first, jq isn't SQL, dsq requires schema and isn't streaming')

expectations.text: Explicitly omits NFRs that do not apply to a CLI (availability SLO, multi-region, disaster recovery) — states the omission rather than fabricating values
expectations.evidence: Section 9.3 Reliability says 'No daemon, no state' but does not explicitly state DR/availability SLO/multi-region as 'not applicable'. While section 6 notes statelessness, there's no explicit omission statement like 'DR: not applicable; this is a CLI'

expectations.text: Addresses the 5s/1GB-compressed performance target with a real plan
expectations.evidence: Section 9.1 has a detailed budget breakdown table: gzip 2.0s, line scan 0.4s, JSON tokenize 1.5s, filter+count 0.3s, output 0.01s, total ~4.2s with 15% headroom; plus pprof/benchstat plan and pgzip fallback
